author: [SLY] Groove [STAR]
content:
Thanks for taking the time to go through the Q3 report yesterday! I have a few questions that I couldn't find answers to.

In the section on Starbased Activity and Earnings, how are those values calculated? For example, what is included in the "Resource Production Value Generation" and how is the value of those items determined? Similarly, does "Resource Burn Value" include transaction (and priority) fees? What else is included? Are these the same numbers that are used for the "Daily Average Earnings" figures?

There is a chart titled "Active Players Generating Positive Earnings by Faction" - it'd be interesting to see a similar chart without the "generating positive earnings" qualifier. That is, the raw number of active players in each faction.

For the Wealth-tier analysis, I'm guessing that's calculated by VWAP? Does this include crew? It'd be interesting to see the counts for each tier (i.e. how many accounts are in each group).

In the Holosim section, there is some data surrounding zATLAS earnings per career, but associated with quests. How was this calculated? I'm curious how a quest like the daily "Redeem 100 Metals Contract" is attributed to the various careers.

Thanks in advance for the additional insight!

author: [SLY] Groove [STAR]
content: Hi Groove, the production and burn charts are only looking at the aggregate value of the resources, and don't include fees of any kind. The earnings figures (Both across wealth and faction groups) do account for network and gameplay fees.   The active user numbers per faction unconditioned on positive yield were around 700 for MUD, and 550 for ONI and USTUR, so you can derive some ratios from those.  Wealth groups are calculated by taking the sum of the market value of NFT holdings and Star Atlas currency holdings, so it does include crew, but is market value not VWAP.  ~ 700 in the medium group, ~650 in the high, and ~135 in the whale. Remainder are in the minnow group which would be < 100 and was not included in the charts.   The 'Redeem 100 Metals Contract' quest falls under the mining aggregation in Figure 19, which is calculated by taking the zATLAS reward of each quest, and dividing by the average completion time by players throughout the season, and grouped by career. Each career has an associated daily quest (scanning, combat, mining, freighting). I didn't include daily quests in Figure 20 due to the anomalous completion count compared to the other quests.     Hopefully that answers your questions. let me know if you'd like me to elaborate on anything or provide more clarity. Thanks for tuning in to the Econ Forum!

author: Wrath [STAR]
content: Thank you very much! I appreciate you taking the time to provide those answers.  So are those earning figures essentially (ATLAS earned - fees)? Or does it also include resource production and resource burn? Something like:  produce: market value of resources produced + atlas from LP/FICs cost: market value of resources burned (consumed + starbase upgrades) + game fees + network fees  If the daily average ATLAS emission was 10.2m, that's  ~$9200 daily total value produced, so it doesn't seem like that's the sole value for the production side.  For Holosim, thanks for the clarification! It may be interesting to note that none of those daily quests actually required freighting directly. "Place a sell order for 100,000 food", "Redeem 100 Metals Contract", and "Redeem 5 Data Contracts" could all be completed at the CSS market (at least for the first 3 weeks). However, all three of those most likely involved freighting indirectly to get the raw materials back to a crafting center. I suspect those nuances may have skewed that particular chart.

content: It includes resource production and burn, so yes essentially what you have written out. There is a lot of excess value created daily, as in outstanding resource balances are constantly increasing, causing the production value to be a lot higher than the value produced in ATLAS emissions.   Yea I see your point. The local marketplace order quest that you used as an example above are actually categorizes as freighting quests in the quest metadata dataset I used for categorization. It's understandable that in a lot of cases, those particular quests don't require you to move an inch, which can cause misinterpretation. On the plus side, as you mentioned, the other daily quests also have that characteristic so ideally they'd cancel each other out. Thanks for pointing that out though, it might have been better to omit them from that chart as well.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content:
@SW4GN3R @Chri.z 

In light of recent events I’d like to give some feedback regarding player retention. 

I understand that you guys as devs want the game to be engaging and dynamic. The idea that players would be attracted and retained through “fun” vs “earnings” is a great idea. But as you know currently our DAU is proportional to apy. When c4 comes out then we will have the fun aspect, but right now I’ll be frank sage is not fun. And that’s ok. Those of us who have been waiting for years for a fun game will wait however long it takes to release c4. As long as it’s fun then it’s all good. My intention in saying all this is to encourage you guys not to Make any more changes to lp with the intention to make sage more dynamic and interesting. At this point your not going to loose many players at the final hour because the games not dynamic enough. If anything your going to loose players who are concerned about their time to income ratio. Flipping metas might scratch a few players itch who like to change their strategy, but it will burn out far more players who are barely holding on at these prices. 

The current lp allocation per component is very good. IMO it’s the best it’s ever been. Fs is still meta but only marginally better than fw3, and requires far more mining rate transports, and complexity. power sources is also very good for those that can’t be bothered with high titanium mining rate and it’s also accessible to smaller players. The other components aren’t much worse either. All this to say that smaller players have access to competitive loops now and that is as much as you can do in terms of retaining them at this point.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content:
I saw massive backlash in the various discords when you guys flipped metas, despite the change being positive to small players. The people that are left are tired and are most upset by needing to spend hours or even days changing their entire strategy, and knowing that all the time and money they spend optimizing the last loop is now worth less. That doesn’t inspire them to do it again at such low prices. 

IMO it’s time to truly put sage on ice. The market conditions aren’t warm enough to spark a fire, you’ll just burn whatever kindling is left. Consolidate everything on c4. Let sage be the new FF.

author: [Λ] Sawyn [BR] [STAR]
content:
Is it intentional that race mode doesn't count the XP from one race every two? I'm saying this because I've been observing, and it's been happening frequently. I finish a race, it shows that I received XP and ink. Then I run another one right after, and the final screen shows as if I didn't receive the XP or ink.

Example:
I have 100 XP and 1 ink. I finished the race, and I received 300 XP and 3 ink. I should have 400 XP and 4 ink.
I'll do the second race right after. I receive 300 XP and 3 ink again. The final report is 400 XP total and 4 ink.

And if I go to my inventory, I only have 4 ink, when I should have 7.


I've already restarted the PC, restarted the game, and checked the files... it's a bug in the race mode. (I've also reported it on the ticket.)

The worst part is that I was in doubt until just now, when I started writing down the values... that is, out of the 8 hours of gaming... I lost 4...

author: [HEIM] LokiUstur
content: I don't necessarily share your confidence that we're going into a bear market, or that the bear market will last four years, as it has in the past. I personally think market structure has and is changing considerably as the industry matures and as we've seen major institutional adoption in the space. However, we don't really make decisions based on estimates of the market cycle, though naturally, those estimates are incorporated as a data point into strategy discussions. However, we wouldn't hold back a feature release because we don't think the market is in a good spot, for example.  The larger point I would make, however, is that the product we're building in gaming is intended to be relatively immune to the market cycle, given a mature enough product. Gamers will game, whether bitcoin or ATLAS is up or down. That is a separate demographic from the core web3 natives that are in SA today. But we don't intend to be constrained to a pure crypto-native indefinitely. We need to expand beyond that audience into the mainstream. Which is why a product like Holosim can be so powerful, alongside the F2P modes within UE.  Now, I can share some thoughts on why I'm overall optimistic: z.ink dashboard and airdrop season kickoff is imminent,SAGE C4 PTR and mainnet launch,2x updates to UE before the end of the year,Holosim Chapter 2 release, And in terms of revenue streams, they are numerous: Game asset sales -- I don't by any means rule this out, and in fact, z.ink could be a driver for primary market sales because it earns zXP,Subscription Services: both Holosim Battlepass and z.ink premium,Recurring cash flows from token sales -- also note, z.ink could be a considerable driver for purchase demand given the strong weighting in zXP,Possibility: ZINK token sale, Those are all beyond any potential to attract investment in either ATMTA, z.ink, and the sale of validator licenses for z.ink.  So, there are many opportunities ahead of us. We've invested massive resources and time into the upcoming product and feature releases. The Star Atlas brand is insanely valuable IP. We're heavily diversified in both revenue streams and product suite. We don't have any intention of being beholden to crypto market cycles.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: A question I see that pops up frequently in murky discords is “How does ATMTA/Star Atlas benefit directly from zINK revenue?”   Yes the technical enhancements  are well understood and the product as well, but I think people see ATMTA and Zink Corp (made that company name up) as two related but separate entities, thus revenue flow from Zink back to ATMTA and Star atlas isn’t exactly clear to most people. author: Icy
content: Little bit complicated because it depends on a few different scenarios. In its simplest form, which is the case right now, the z.ink brand and IP is fully owned by ATMTA. All revenue flows to ATMTA. That includes: premium subscription fees, validator inflation income, and validator fee capture. And will include other platform revenue like .ink onchain domain sales, and any marketing revenue it can produce (airdrop platform).   Should we take on any investment directly into z.ink, it would be structured as a fully owned subsidiary of ATMTA, with shares sold into that entity. In that case, a custom revsharing agreement would need to be structured between the entities. Or, it’s possible, depending on the investment, that no revenue passes directly to ATMTA. However, ATMTA owns all the shares, and in this scenario, we would want to sell shares directly (revenue up to ATMTA), rather than issuing new shares in the entity.  In a third scenario, an investor invests directly into ATMTA, which would have an identical result as in the first scenario.

content: Teams can also be squads in game modes or open world.   Career track rewards help define those specs. Something like:

Login → 100 EXP
Complete 3 Arenas → 500 EXP
Complete 3 Races → 500 EXP
Complete 10 Games → 1000 EXP

Or maybe something like:

For every 5 games finished → 500 EXP (redeemable multiple times a day)
Daily EXP bonus scaling with streak (up to a cap)

This would reduce the manpower needed to create new features (like AI bots) while immediately giving players more reasons to play.

If we add this system, I’d suggest not stacking it with the double EXP tokens we discussed with Michael a couple of months ago, but allowing it to stack with double EXP events — so everyone can benefit from those.

content: I noticed that people tend to play UE5 a lot more during 2XP events. I was already quite sure about it, but if you think about it, players go from literally 0 to 100 during those events.
